items.title: ChatGPT by OpenAI - Release Notes - March 2026 - Releasebot
items.author: releasebot.io
items.snippet: OpenAI has been active in March 2026 with a series of major announcements across funding, models, and product updates. The company secured a record-breaking $110 billion funding round led by Amazon, Nvidia, and SoftBank, alongside a $100 billion extension on its AWS compute capacity agreement. On the model front, OpenAI released three GPT-5.4 variants (Standard, Thinking, and Pro) during a particularly active week (March 10–16) that saw coordinated launches across the AI industry. items.title: ChatGPT Enterprise & Edu - Release Notes | OpenAI Help Center
items.author: help.openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has continued its aggressive model release cadence into early 2026. The most notable recent launches include GPT-5.3-Codex and GPT-5.4, with GPT-5.3-Codex positioning itself as OpenAI's most capable agentic coding model — combining the Codex and GPT-5 training stacks, running ~25% faster than predecessors, and excelling at long-running tasks involving research, tool use, and complex multi-step execution. Users can interact with and steer the model mid-task without losing context, making i

items.title: OpenAI Research Publications | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI published several notable research papers in March 2026. On March 19, they released research on 'Evaluating Chain-of-Thought Monitorability,' examining how chain-of-thought reasoning in coding agents can be monitored for signs of misalignment — a key safety research focus. On March 10, they published work on improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs via a method called IH-Challenge, which trains models to prioritize trusted instructions and resist prompt injection attacks. On March
